reforms.description: The government announced measures to support enterprise creation, including the elimination of all state taxes on enterprise creation and the provision of new credits for enterprises in new technology and proximity areas.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The elimination of taxes and provision of credits are expected to incentivize new enterprise creation, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been put into effect as part of the government's support for enterprise creation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The measures represent a significant but not structural change in supporting enterprise creation.
reforms.source_quote: The Prime Minister announces measures to support enterprise creation: all state taxes on enterprise creation are eliminated; new credits will be provided to enterprises working in the new technology and proximity areas.

reforms.description: In 1999, France introduced two ecological taxes aimed at reducing water pollution, which are part of the General Tax on Polluting Activities (TGAP). These taxes target detergent phosphates and agricultural pesticides, with revenues intended to support environmental initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The ecological taxes are designed to fund environmental initiatives, which can lead to innovation in sustainable practices and technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The ecological taxes have been enacted and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of ecological taxes represents a significant policy change aimed at environmental sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: In 1999, two ecological taxes aimed at reducing water pollution were created and included in the General Tax on Polluting Activities (TGAP).
